REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS BRIAN REED, 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET HOMEOWNERS' MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT (CODE CIV. PROC. 425.16)IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN AND ESTABLISH THAT ANY CLAIM ADVANCED IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. EACH OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS SPECIFICALLY STATES THE BASIS (E.G. PARAGRAPH 41) AND DOES NOT ARISE OUT OF ANY "ORAL OR WRITTEN STATEMENTS." FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANT'S EMPLOY AN IMPROPER ANALYSIS OF THE APPLICABILITY OF ANTI-SLAPP. SPECIFICALLY, DEFENDANT REFERENCES "THE GRAVAMEN OF THE COMPLAINT," A CONCEPT ABANDONED SINCE BARAL V. SCHNITT (2016) 1 CAL.5TH 376. WHILE DEFENDANT RELIES ON LEE V. SILVEIRA (2016) 6 CAL.APP.5TH 527, DAMON V. OCEAN HILLS JOURNALIST CLUB (2000) 85 CAL.APP.4TH 468, AND SCHROEDER V. IRVINE CITY COUNCIL (2002) 97 CAL.APP.4TH 174 DEFENDANT FAILED TO IDENTIFY A SINGLE CLAIM IN THE COMPLAINT THAT ARISES OUT OF THE CONTENT OF THE VOTE, OR STATEMENTS MADE DURING THE MEETINGS, ETC. ORDER FOR THE SAME SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Continued To Nov-08-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs BRIAN REED, and 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET HOMEOWNERS' Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action is continued to November 8, 2019 for supplemental briefing. By no later than October 25, 2019 Defendants to file a supplemental brief addressing:

1. Whether criteria used for determination of applicability of arbitration clause to statutory claims is relevant to the Unruh and FEHA claims (Opposition 5:4-10); and assuming that it is whether the CC&Rs' arbitration clause meets the requirements.
2. Whether discovery propounded by Defendant was limited solely to the issues relevant to opposition to the motion for preliminary injunction. Defendant shall specifically address whether discovery was served in sufficient time to obtain responses prior to opposition to the preliminary injunction due date and whether any of the responses were used to support opposition.
3. Whether filing an ANTI-SLAPP is constitutes "invoking the litigation machinery."
The brief shall be limited to the issues listed above. The entire brief will be stricken if its scope exceeds issues 1-3.

Plaintiff may file a responsive brief by no later than November 1, 2019.Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Allyssa Villanueva, esq. (510-897-9966) of Benjamin Law Group vfor Plaintiff.
Edward J. Reid, esq. (714-918-7000) of Hall Griffin for Defendant.
Kate A. Golovanova, esq. (510-834-4350) of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe for Defendants.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed:
Preliminary Injunction is Lifted (request to lift the injunction has previously been denied without prejudice and is renewed by the Defendants). Events and multiple hearings surrounding the proper functioning of the HOA and the impact it has on its members show that the balance of harms in this case (one of the two elements required for a preliminary injunction) dramatically favors the lifting of the injunction. Order to Show Cause is hereby discharged and the preliminary injunction is lifted. The HOA may proceed with all of its operations, including those previously enjoined.

Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Allyssa Villanueva, Esq. of Benjamin Law Group, 510-897-9966, appearing for Olusoji Fanoiki, Plaintiff and moving party.

Alan Swerdlow, Esq. of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe, 510-834-4350, appearing for Brian Reed and 1081-1087 Mississippi Street, Defendant and moving party.

Edward Reid, Esq. of Boornazian, Jensen & Garthe, 510-834-4350, appearing via Court Call and for Brian Reed and 1081-1087 Mississippi Street, Defendant and moving party.

ARGUED. Mr. Swerdlow stated that he contests to the tentative, that Mr. Fanoiki is destroying the property, that there is garbage in the hallways, that there is damage to the roof and that everyone has to pay except for Mr. Fanoiki. Ms. Villanueva argued that her client is disputing the amounts being assessed to him and requested that the amount of $11,500 be due on Friday 2/15/19. Counsel to give notice once the amount is posted.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the modified tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF OLUSOJI FANOIKI Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Prohibit Foreclosure Sale is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall post with the Court cash or bond in the amount of $11,500 no later than February 15, 2019 by 2:00 p.m. Plaintiff shall also pay all regular and special assessments due and owing (excluding those in dispute on this motion). If any assessment payments are currently past due, the payment shall be made by 2:00 p.m. on February 13, 2019. All future regular and special assessments to be paid in a timely fashion for the duration of the injunction. Injunction shall take effect upon timely posting of the bond and timely payment of the due and owing assessment (to the exclusion of those specified in the joint brief). Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16)
Off Calendar Jan-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
(510-897-9976) Allysa Villanueva for Plaintiff/Moving party .

(510-834-4350) Alan Swerdlow appearing for Defendant/opposing party.

(714-918-7000) Edward Reid for Defendant/opposing party.

ARGUED. Mr. Swerdlow stated that this anti slap lawsuit should be dismissed, that this is from a disgruntled tenant, that he failed to pay assessment and provided further case background. Ms. Villanueva reported that her client never violated any HOA rules, the his rights were violated and he was unable to dispute the fees and fines given to him. Mr. Reid spoke about the chilling effect of Fanoiki's behavior.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS BRIAN REED, 1081-1087 MISSISSIPPI STREET HOMEOWNERS' Motion And Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint (Code Civ. Proc. 425.16)is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and establish that any claim advanced in Plaintiff's complaint arises out of protected activity. Each of Plaintiff's claims specifically states the basis (e.g. paragraph 41) and does not arise out of any "oral or written statements." Furthermore, Defendant's employ an improper analysis of the applicability of Anti-SLAPP. Specifically, Defendant references "the gravamen of the complaint," a concept abandoned since Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. While Defendant relies on Lee v. Silveira (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 527, Damon v. Ocean Hills Journalist Club (2000) 85 Cal.app.4th 468, and Schroeder v. Irvine City Council (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 174 Defendant failed to identify a single claim in the complaint that arises out of the content of the vote, or statements made during the meetings, etc. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
